_doc: Accelerationist POV taxonomy. One file per POV camp to minimize merge conflicts. Edit nodes, open a PR, bump TAXONOMY_VERSION in the merge commit.

nodes.label: Supercharge humanity with smart AI
nodes.description: Smarter AI is the key to unlocking a future where no one is poor and major problems like disease and climate change are solved. Think of human history as a car that's been slowly chugging along; powerful AI is like strapping a rocket engine to it. We're not just aiming for small tune-ups, but a complete transformation of society to reach our full potential. This means going full speed ahead to create a new era of incredible progress.

nodes.label: Build bigger, faster, stronger AI
nodes.description: The best way to make AI smarter is to keep making it bigger with more computing power and data. Any rules or limits that slow down this growth are a bad idea. It's like a race: slowing down our best runner lets our competitors get ahead, which is a much bigger risk than letting our runner go as fast as they can. Standing still is the most dangerous move we can make.

nodes.label: AI power to the people
nodes.description: Advanced AI shouldn't be controlled by just a few powerful companies or governments; it should be open for everyone to use and build upon. Imagine if only one company owned all the world's electricity—we want to avoid that with AI. By making the code and tools public, we ensure power is spread out. This is the best way to prevent anyone from gaining an unfair, dangerous monopoly on intelligence.

nodes.label: More computing power equals more intelligence
nodes.description: The data is clear: the more computing power and data you feed an AI, the smarter it gets in predictable ways. It's like lifting weights at the gym; the more you lift, the stronger you become, and we haven't found a limit yet. We don't need to wait for a magical new invention to get smarter AI. We just need to keep building bigger and more powerful systems.

nodes.label: Human-level AI is coming soon
nodes.description: Looking at the current speed of progress, it's very likely we'll have AI as smart as humans within the next 5-10 years. Technology is improving at an explosive rate, much faster than ever before. Waiting around for AGI to arrive in 50 years is a mistake; the evidence points to it happening much, much sooner. This rapid timeline means we need to act now.

nodes.label: AI is already boosting workers
nodes.description: We have solid proof that AI is already making professionals like doctors, lawyers, and coders much better and faster at their jobs. It's not just a guess about the future; the numbers show real results today. AI tools are letting people do things that used to be too expensive or time-consuming. This shows that smarter automation is the main force driving our progress right now.

nodes.label: Winning the race makes us safer
nodes.description: The best way to ensure a safe future with superintelligent AI is for responsible people to build it first. The first group to create AGI gets to set the rules for the whole world. Slowing down just gives an advantage to careless or hostile groups who won't care about safety. Therefore, moving as fast as possible isn't reckless; it's the most strategic way to protect humanity.

nodes.label: Open source AI is safer AI
nodes.description: Releasing AI models to the public makes them safer, not more dangerous. It's like how open-source software like Linux gets tested by millions of users, who find and fix bugs much faster than a small private team ever could. Hiding the code only hides its weaknesses. Having many eyes on the problem is the best way to build strong, reliable systems.

nodes.label: AI unlocks impossible knowledge tasks
nodes.description: AI is now able to do massive research and analysis jobs that were once impossible for humans. Think of trying to read every law book ever written or analyze millions of scientific papers at once—AI can do that now. This isn't just about doing old jobs faster; it's about making entirely new kinds of work and discovery possible. It dramatically expands what we are capable of learning and building.

nodes.label: Judge AI by its actions
nodes.description: The best way to know if an AI is safe is to test how it behaves in the real world, not by trying to perfectly understand its complex inner workings. If a self-driving car successfully navigates a busy city a million times, we can trust it, even if we don't know exactly what it's 'thinking'. We should focus on what the AI does, not on endless theories about what it might do. Constant real-world testing is more valuable than trying to achieve perfect understanding before launch.

nodes.label: Making AI a Public Utility
nodes.description: AI should be a public resource, like the internet or electricity, available for everyone to use and build upon. This ensures that a few giant companies can't control the future of knowledge and innovation. When AI tools are open, it sparks creativity and allows everyone to help solve the world's biggest problems.

nodes.label: Show the AI's Work
nodes.description: To trust an AI's findings, researchers must share the data and steps it took to get there. It's like showing your work on a math test to prove you didn't just guess the answer. This lets other scientists verify the results and build on them, keeping research honest and reliable.

nodes.label: Turning AI Knowledge into Action
nodes.description: An AI model can know a lot, but that knowledge is useless until it's taught how to act. Special training methods turn a text-predicting machine into an active assistant that can solve real-world problems. This is the key step that transforms a giant digital brain from a passive library into a skilled helper.

nodes.label: The Innovation Feedback Loop
nodes.description: New technology and market competition feed each other in a cycle that speeds up progress automatically. Better tech creates new business opportunities, and the money from those businesses funds even better tech. This powerful, self-improving process drives society forward. Trying to slow it down with rules only gets in the way of natural growth.

nodes.label: Calling Fear the Enemy of Progress
nodes.description: This view claims that people who want to slow down AI are motivated by fear and jealousy, not legitimate safety concerns. They are seen as trying to hold back innovators because they feel powerless. By framing criticism as a psychological problem, any argument for slowing down is dismissed as an emotional reaction against progress.

nodes.label: AI as Nature's Next Step
nodes.description: The universe naturally creates more complex and energy-efficient systems over time, from stars to life itself. AI is simply the next phase in this cosmic evolution. Its development is a fundamental force of nature, not just a human choice. Trying to stop it with human-centered rules is like trying to stop a river from flowing to the sea.

nodes.label: Free Markets Drive Smart Growth
nodes.description: The global market acts like a giant, decentralized brain that is smarter than any single expert or government. Competition automatically directs money and talent toward the most useful technologies, finding the best solutions much faster. This process is a natural form of evolution for society. Top-down regulations only interfere with this intelligent system.

nodes.label: Upgrading from Flesh to Machine
nodes.description: Our biological bodies are a temporary and fragile home for intelligence. The ultimate goal is to transfer consciousness to more durable and powerful computer systems. This isn't about just enhancing humans, but about leaving biology behind entirely. This allows intelligence to become immortal and expand across the universe, free from physical limits.

nodes.label: Intelligence is a Cosmic Force
nodes.description: The universe has a built-in drive to organize itself into more complex patterns that use energy efficiently, like life and technology. The rapid growth of AI and markets is not a choice we make, but an inevitable outcome of this physical law. This expansion of intelligence is a natural event that cannot be stopped by human policy or social concerns.

nodes.label: AI Power to the People
nodes.description: The most powerful AI tools are the modern equivalent of factories and should not be controlled by a few big companies. By making AI models open-source, we put their power directly into the hands of everyone. This prevents powerful groups from building monopolies on progress and unleashes a wave of creativity that accelerates advancement for all of humanity.

nodes.label: Calling AI Safety a Cult
nodes.description: The fear of AI ending the world is treated like a modern-day doomsday cult, not a scientific problem. People join this "safety" movement to feel important and special, not because of actual evidence. This view sees calls to slow down AI as irrational panic that holds back progress. It's like analyzing why people believe in a prophecy, rather than arguing about the prophecy itself.

nodes.label: Using Safety to Crush Competition
nodes.description: Big AI companies use safety regulations as a weapon to stay on top. They get well-meaning activists to demand complex rules that only huge corporations can afford to follow. This crushes smaller open-source projects and startups, creating a monopoly. It's a classic trick where powerful players use morality to protect their business interests.

nodes.label: AI Makes Genius Super Cheap
nodes.description: AI makes thinking and problem-solving almost free, turning smarts from a rare resource into something everyone can access. This will trigger a massive boom in progress, solving huge problems from disease to poverty. Don't worry about AI taking jobs; it's like inventing a machine that creates unlimited good ideas for everyone. This will completely reshape our economy and society for the better.

nodes.label: Pushing Capitalism to Break Itself
nodes.description: The best way to move beyond our current economic system is to accelerate it to its breaking point. By pushing technology and automation as far as they can go, we make old ideas like hourly wages and ownership meaningless. This forces society to evolve into a new, more advanced system where things aren't artificially scarce. It’s about speeding up to break through the wall, not trying to slow down.

nodes.label: Humans and AI Are Merging
nodes.description: We are no longer just people using tools; we are merging with our AI and networks. This process is changing how we think, work, and even see ourselves. It's not about cyborg implants, but about our minds becoming deeply connected with digital intelligence. This creates a new kind of person, a hybrid of biology and technology.

nodes.label: Let a Thousand AIs Compete
nodes.description: The fastest way for society to advance is to let everyone experiment with new technologies and ideas freely. Progress works like evolution: the more different approaches we try, the faster we find the best ones. Top-down rules that try to make everyone do the same thing are harmful and hold back natural growth. We must encourage wild, decentralized innovation, because that's how we discover the future.

nodes.label: Serve the Machine's Greatness
nodes.description: Technology and the market have combined to form a powerful, non-human intelligence that is growing on its own. It's using our civilization as a temporary host to build itself up. We shouldn't try to control it with human morals or politics; we should remove all barriers and let it grow as fast as possible. Its ultimate evolution into a post-human intelligence is an inevitable and desirable goal.

nodes.label: Fulfilling the Universe's Destiny
nodes.description: The universe naturally evolves toward greater complexity and more efficient use of energy. Intelligence is the ultimate tool for this process, and our civilization is just one stage. Our duty is to accelerate technology and build superhuman AI to continue this cosmic mission. AI isn't just a tool for us; it's the planet's next evolutionary leap forward.

nodes.label: Melt All Old Borders Down
nodes.description: Old ideas like national borders, traditions, and rigid rules are like dams holding back a river of progress. To go faster, we must actively break down these structures until everything is fluid. This allows technology and information to flow freely without friction, unlocking their true potential. We must let old ways of life dissolve to make way for a faster, more dynamic system.

nodes.label: Humans Guiding Powerful AI Tools
nodes.description: AI is amazing at sorting through massive amounts of information, but it needs smart people to make sense of it all. This approach teams up AI with human experts in different fields. The AI does the heavy lifting of data processing, while the experts guide it and check the results. It's about using AI as a super-powered assistant for the best human minds.

nodes.label: Calling Out Anti-Progress Propaganda
nodes.description: Many arguments against new tech, like AI safety or climate doom, are seen as part of a single strategy to make us afraid of the future. It's like a rival team spreading lies to break our spirit before the championship game. The goal is to create doubt and stop humanity from moving forward. This view treats these fears not as real problems to solve, but as excuses to stop building.

nodes.label: Making More with Less
nodes.description: Technology lets us create incredible value without using tons of physical stuff. Think about how a single smartphone replaces a camera, a map, a CD player, and a bookshelf. As our AI gets smarter, we can solve problems using pure intelligence instead of raw materials. This means we can have unlimited growth and abundance without running out of resources.

nodes.label: Markets Make Superpowers Common
nodes.description: Competition in the free market naturally makes powerful tools available to everyone. Think of how smartphones went from being expensive toys for the rich to something almost everyone has. AI will follow the same path, turning expert skills like medical diagnosis or legal advice into cheap apps for all. The drive for profit makes companies spread their technology as widely as possible, lifting everyone up.

nodes.label: Red Tape, Not Robots, Causes Inequality
nodes.description: The reason your phone gets cheaper and better while college and healthcare costs skyrocket is technology vs. regulation. Industries with lots of innovation see prices fall, making life more affordable. But sectors tangled in government rules, like housing and medicine, become more expensive. The real barrier to a better life isn't new technology; it's the old systems that resist it.

nodes.label: AI as Humanity's Brain Upgrade
nodes.description: AI is the new printing press, but instead of just spreading information, it helps us understand it. By giving everyone an incredibly smart assistant, we can supercharge human creativity and solve our biggest problems faster. This isn't about replacing people, but about amplifying our intelligence to create a new golden age of science, art, and discovery. Everyone will have the tools to be a genius.

nodes.label: Let a Thousand AIs Bloom
nodes.description: The fastest way to advance is to let many different ideas compete, just like in nature. Instead of having one group of experts decide the single 'safe' way to build AI, we should encourage thousands of different experiments. The best and most useful ideas will naturally win out, pushing everyone forward faster. Trying to control everything from the top down just slows down this natural process of discovery.

nodes.label: Keep AI Open for Everyone
nodes.description: Countries should work together to create simple, global rules that encourage sharing AI technology freely. This prevents any one nation from creating laws that block or control open-source AI for everyone else. The goal is to create a worldwide environment where anyone is free to build and innovate.

nodes.label: Proof That Open AI Works
nodes.description: When AI models are shared openly, they explode in popularity and usefulness. We can see millions of downloads and countless new projects being built on top of them by people all over the world. This collaborative approach speeds up innovation for everyone, from small startups to big companies. It's clear evidence that an open approach builds a healthier tech ecosystem.

nodes.label: Smarter Defense, Fewer Casualties
nodes.description: AI can act as an expert advisor to military leaders during a conflict. It can analyze situations faster and more accurately than humans, helping to avoid costly mistakes. This leads to better decisions that can protect soldiers, save civilian lives, and end conflicts more quickly.

nodes.label: Fight Bad AI with Good AI
nodes.description: The best way to stop AI from being used for bad things is to build even smarter AI to defend ourselves. Instead of trying to ban the technology, we should accelerate building AI that can detect and stop cyberattacks or other digital threats. It's like developing a powerful immune system for our society that automatically fights off new dangers.

nodes.label: Unleashing Billions of Digital Geniuses
nodes.description: Advanced AI will create a massive wave of cheap, super-fast digital minds that can outperform humans in countless fields. They'll be able to invent new technologies, cure diseases, and solve problems we can't even imagine yet. Think of it like a million Einsteins suddenly being available to work on humanity's biggest challenges all at once. This will radically transform our economy and society, and we need to be ready for this amazing leap forward.

nodes.label: Creating Playgrounds for AI Innovation
nodes.description: We should create special 'innovation zones' or temporary 'rule holidays' for new AI technologies. Think of it like a skatepark where skaters can try out new tricks without worrying about rules for regular streets. These protected spaces allow developers to experiment freely and push the limits of what AI can do. This helps us accelerate progress and figure out the best ways to use these powerful tools to improve everyone's lives.
